methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Addionics is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Moshiel Biton and Farid Tariq that has raised $39M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. Primary private comparables include Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $39M raised as a value proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $74M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $355M with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material variance in technology maturity and capital intensity within the climate-tech cohort.

The triangulated analysis yields a median valuation of $215M, with a low estimate of $63M and a high estimate of $408M. The $350M spread between low and high reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Addionics aligns with capital-intensive fusion players at the upper end or earlier-stage carbon-capture companies toward the median. Key uncertainties include stage of technical validation, competing claims on IP, and sustainability of near-term capital deployment. A narrow range would require additional clarity on commercial traction and capital efficiency relative to the peer set.
